content:
Architectural design, user roles, domain entities, and roadmap for a modern barber shop and hair salon management web platform.

3 min read
Just now

--

Welcome to the first progress update for DaviWeb, a full-stack web application designed to optimize appointment scheduling and service management for hair salons, barber shops, and personal care centers.
This project is being developed as a Final Degree Thesis (TFG) for the Software Engineering Degree at Universidad Rey Juan Carlos (URJC), supervised by Professor Óscar Soto Sánchez.
— — —
1. Project Overview and Core Goals
Managing appointments, employee shifts, and client interactions in service-oriented businesses often suffers from manual booking errors and unoptimized schedules. DaviWeb solves this by offering a dual-perspective platform:

• For Clients: A smooth, intuitive interface to browse services, check real-time availability, select preferred stylists, and book appointments adapted to their hair length and requirements.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

• For Business Owners: A comprehensive administration dashboard to manage working schedules, active services, employee availability, no-show tracking, and business analytics.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

— — —
2. Architecture & Tech Stack
To ensure scalability, modularity, and high performance, DaviWeb relies on a modern cloud-ready stack:

• Frontend: Single Page Application (SPA) providing a fast and responsive user experience.

• Backend: Java with Spring Boot providing a robust RESTful API.

• Database: PostgreSQL for relational data persistence.

• Cloud Infrastructure:
— AWS RDS: Managed PostgreSQL database instance.
— AWS S3: Object storage for dynamically uploaded user profile pictures, employee headshots, and service thumbnails.

• DevOps & CI/CD: Docker and Docker Compose for containerized local execution, combined with GitHub Actions for automated build and test pipelines.
— — —
3. User Roles & Access Control
The platform enforces Role-Based Access Control (RBAC) divided into three key user profiles:
1. Anonymous Visitor: Browses the public catalog, location map, FAQs, public team members, and daily gallery photos.
2. Registered Client: Logs in to schedule appointments, manage profile settings, review booking history, and cancel or modify upcoming slots.
3. Administrator: Accesses private dashboards for catalog customization, schedule and holiday exceptions, staff management, blacklist/no-show logging, and business metrics charts.
— — —
4. Key Domain Entities & Advanced Algorithms
The core backend domain revolves around four main relational entities: User, Service, Employee, and Appointment.
One of the highlight features of DaviWeb is its Dynamic Time-Slot Availability Algorithm. Services in the system are not static; they account for:

• Hair Length Modifiers: Execution times dynamically scale depending on hair length (short, medium, long).

• Time Segments: Breakdown of active service time and automatic buffer/break intervals between appointments.

• Schedule Restrictions: Enforcing operating hours, specific employee availability, and special holiday overrides without double-booking.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

— — —
5. Roadmap & Summer Development Plan
The project follows an iterative methodology structured into 7 milestones:

• Phase 1 (Requirements & UI Design): Core specifications, mockups, and domain definition.

• Phase 2 (Repository & CI/CD): Base project structure, unit tests, and GitHub Actions workflow.

• Phase 3 (Version 0.1 — MVP): Basic frontend-backend integration, auth, dockerization, and simple booking flow.

• Phase 4 (Version 0.2 — Intermediate): Service catalog CRUD, administrative schedules, and gallery management.

• Phase 5 (Version 1.0 — Advanced): AWS S3 integration, analytics dashboard (Charts), direct cancellation tokens, and automated email notifications.

• Phases 6 and 7: Technical documentation writing and academic thesis defense.
